_README: TAPPaaS satellite module config (ADR-010). An EXTERNAL host (kind=external-host) that tappaas-cicd provisions via nixos-anywhere and manages with `satellite-manager` — NOT a Proxmox cluster:vm. This file exposes ONLY the operator-facing choices; ALL derived values (tunnel /31 + ports, per-role tuning, backup mechanics, update mode) are computed with sensible defaults by satellite-manager at install time (see lib/provision.sh). Copy to ~/config/satellite-<name>.json and edit. Secrets are never stored here.
description: Operator-owned VPS satellite: reverse-proxy frontend, admin VPN, and off-site backup for a cluster with no public IP
name: satellite
_dependsOn_note: Empty by design: the satellite is external-host and operator-driven (satellite-manager), NOT installed through install-module.sh, so it does not participate in the module dependency graph. Its real prerequisites — the `network` module (OPNsense) up, `tappaas-cicd` up, and the `backup` module (home PBS) for the backup role — are checked by satellite-manager at install time, not resolved here.

provider._type_values: hetzner | generic (operator supplies a host + SSH; no API automation)
provider._allocation_values: portal (Tier A, default) | api (Tier B, opt-in: hcloud API token)

host._publicIp_note: EXAMPLE placeholder — 203.0.113.10 is RFC 5737 TEST-NET-3. Replace with the real satellite public IP.

backup._note: Only used when 'backup' is in roles. The operator supplies the S3 target; everything else (pull direction, --remove-vanished false, client-side encryption, S3 Object-Lock + retention, namespace) is a sensible default in satellite-manager. Omit this block for a relay/admin-only satellite. For the dedicated-volume backend instead of S3, set backend:'volume' (advanced).
